200.q: What family says Hear Me Roar?
200.a: House Lannister

200.q: What is the ancestral Stark sword called?
200.a: Ice

200.q: What city is the capital of the Seven Kingdoms?
200.a: King's Landing

200.q: What is Jon Snow's direwolf named?
200.a: Ghost
Arya Stark

200.q: What noble house has the direwolf as its sigil?
200.a: House Stark
A three-headed dragon
Winter is Coming
Olenna Tyrell — she poisoned the wine at the Purple Wedding
Drogon, Rhaegal, and Viserion

200.q: Who sits on the Iron Throne at the end?
200.a: Bran Stark (the Broken)

200.q: What is Arya Stark's sword called?
200.a: Needle

200.q: What is Jon Snow's real parentage?
200.a: He is Aegon Targaryen — son of Rhaegar Targaryen and Lyanna Stark
Arya Stark
All men must die (in High Valyrian)
The leader of the White Walkers — created by the Children of the Forest

200.q: What is the Iron Throne made of?
200.a: Swords of defeated enemies — melted and fused with dragon fire
Lord of Winterfell — beheaded in Season 1 after being accused of treason
A massacre at Walder Frey's castle where Robb Stark and allies are slaughtered
Bran's gentle giant servant — his entire life leads to the tragic moment of Hold the door
An extremely rare sharp metal — one of the only materials that kills White Walkers
Cersei's younger brother — the imp, known for his intelligence and wit
A massive ice barrier in the North protecting the Seven Kingdoms from what lies beyond
He is a warg and greenseer — able to see past, present, and future events
Littlefinger — a cunning political schemer who orchestrated much of the chaos in Westeros
The Kingslayer — who killed the Mad King and is conflicted throughout the series
From naive girl to shrewd political operator — she ends as Queen in the North
The Red Priestess who serves the Lord of Light — she resurrects Jon Snow
Obsidian — one of the only materials that kills White Walkers
Robert's brother who claims the Iron Throne — a rigid leader who burns his own daughter
Jon Snow's albino direwolf

200.q: What is House Lannister's unofficial motto?
200.a: A Lannister always pays his debts

400.q: What is the poison used to kill Joffrey commonly called?
400.a: The Strangler

400.q: Who is known as the Onion Knight?
400.a: Davos Seaworth

400.q: What is the seat of House Arryn?
400.a: The Eyrie

400.q: What is Brienne's full title after she is knighted?
400.a: Ser Brienne of Tarth

400.q: What city is the seat of House Tyrell?
400.a: Highgarden

400.q: Which dragon is Daenerys's largest and fiercest?
400.a: Drogon

400.q: What castle is the ancestral seat of House Arryn?
400.a: The Eyrie

400.q: What metal can kill White Walkers as discovered by Sam?
400.a: Dragonglass

400.q: What title does Tyrion hold under Daenerys?
400.a: Hand of the Queen

400.q: What is the name of Arya's Faceless Man trainer?
400.a: Jaqen H'ghar

400.q: Who betrayed Robb Stark at the Red Wedding?
400.a: Roose Bolton and Walder Frey — orchestrated by Tywin Lannister

400.q: What house motto is We Do Not Sow?
400.a: House Greyjoy of the Iron Islands

400.q: Who was the Mad King?
400.a: Aerys II Targaryen — killed by Jaime Lannister

400.q: What is the origin of the Night King?
400.a: A First Man transformed by the Children of the Forest with a dragonglass dagger

400.q: Who is Cersei's children's real father?
400.a: Jaime Lannister — her twin brother

400.q: What is the significance of the Battle of the Bastards?
400.a: Jon Snow vs Ramsay Bolton — won by the Knights of the Vale arriving at the last moment

400.q: Who is Ramsay Bolton?
400.a: The sadistic son of Roose Bolton — he captures and tortures Theon, and rapes Sansa

400.q: What is the Sept of Baelor explosion?
400.a: Cersei uses wildfire to destroy her enemies — killing Margaery, the High Sparrow, and many others

400.q: What is the Three-Eyed Raven?
400.a: An ancient being who holds all memories of the world — Bran becomes the new one

400.q: Who is Samwell Tarly?
400.a: Jon's best friend — discovers dragonglass kills White Walkers and uncovers Jon's true parentage

400.q: What happened to Daenerys in the final season?
400.a: She burns King's Landing — Jon kills her, Drogon destroys the Iron Throne

400.q: Who is Euron Greyjoy?
400.a: Cersei's pirate-king ally — he commands the Iron Fleet and kills two of Daenerys's dragons

400.q: What is the significance of the Tower of Joy flashback?
400.a: Reveals Jon is not Ned's son — he is the son of Rhaegar Targaryen and Lyanna Stark

400.q: What is Varys's role?
400.a: The Spider — spymaster who ultimately tries to betray Daenerys to Jon

400.q: Who is Olenna Tyrell?
400.a: The Queen of Thorns — matriarch of House Tyrell known for her wit and cunning

400.q: What happens to the Iron Throne?
400.a: Drogon melts it after Jon kills Daenerys — it is destroyed

400.q: Who is Theon Greyjoy's arc?
400.a: He betrays Robb Stark, is tortured by Ramsay as Reek, then redeems himself at the Battle of Winterfell

400.q: What is the Loot Train Attack?
400.a: Daenerys attacks the Lannister forces with Drogon and the Dothraki — they are devastated

400.q: Who is Jorah Mormont?
400.a: Daenerys's loyal protector who loves her — he dies protecting her at Winterfell

400.q: What is the Catspaw dagger?
400.a: The Valyrian steel dagger Arya uses to kill the Night King

400.q: What are the Faceless Men?
400.a: A guild of assassins from Braavos who serve the Many-Faced God — Arya trains with them

400.q: Who is the Iron Bank of Braavos?
400.a: The most powerful financial institution in the world — it backed Stannis and later Cersei

400.q: What is the Lord of Light?
400.a: R'hllor — the god worshipped by Melisandre and Thoros, associated with fire and resurrection

400.q: What is the Free Folk?
400.a: People who live beyond the Wall, also called Wildlings — Jon brings them south of the Wall

400.q: What is House Targaryen's words?
400.a: Fire and Blood

600.q: What are the words of House Martell?
What valyrian steel dagger starts many major plotlines?
What city does Daenerys rule before sailing to Westeros?
What is Hodor's real name?
What island seat belongs to House Greyjoy?
What Valyrian steel blade kills the Night King?
What are House Greyjoy's words?
What is the ancestral Stark sword called?
What island fortress becomes Daenerys's base in season 7?
What is the Valyrian steel dagger used against Bran later known for killing?
What family holds the Twins?
What is the name of Sansa's direwolf?
What order protects the Wall?
What is the Hound's real first name?
What free city is famous for its canals and Faceless Men?
What mercenary company does Daario Naharis lead?
Who is revealed as the Kingslayer?
What is the name of Bran Stark's direwolf?
What Valyrian steel dagger triggers many plotlines?
What is the name of Arya Stark's sword?
What poisonous substance kills Joffrey?
Name all members of Ned Stark's Small Council in Season 1.
600.a: Hand: Ned Stark; Coin: Littlefinger; Whisperers: Varys; Grand Maester: Pycelle; Ships: Stannis; Laws: Renly

600.q: What is the Azor Ahai legend?
600.a: Azor Ahai forged Lightbringer by plunging it into his wife's heart — linked to the hero defeating the Long Night

600.q: What prophecy haunts Cersei?
600.a: The Valonqar: a younger sibling will wrap hands around her pale white throat and choke the life from her

600.q: What is the significance of Daenerys's Targaryen madness?
600.a: The show implies madness runs in Targaryen blood — she shows it by burning King's Landing despite them surrendering

600.q: What is the Long Night?
600.a: An ancient winter lasting a generation — the first war against the White Walkers thousands of years ago

600.q: Who is Bloodraven?
600.a: Brynden Rivers — a bastard of House Targaryen who became a ranger then the Three-Eyed Raven

600.q: What is the Valyrian Doom?
600.a: A cataclysmic event that destroyed the Valyrian Freehold — the Targaryens escaped because they had a prophecy

600.q: What is the meaning of Dracarys?
600.a: Dragonfire in High Valyrian — the command Daenerys uses to order her dragons to breathe fire

600.q: What is the Faceless Men's true belief?
600.a: All gods are faces of the Many-Faced God of Death — and they serve by giving the gift of death

600.q: What is the Prince That Was Promised prophecy?
600.a: A hero born of smoke and salt who will save the world — both Jon and Daenerys are possible candidates

600.q: What is the significance of Cersei destroying the Sept?
600.a: She eliminates all her political enemies at once — but loses Tommen (her last child) who jumps from a window in grief

600.q: What is the Night King's ultimate goal?
600.a: To destroy the Three-Eyed Raven and erase all human memory, then overwhelm the world with his undead army
